data.description:

Inter & Company Incorporation Class A ( (INTR) ) has shared an announcement.

Inter & Co, Inc. held its 2026 Annual General Meeting on April 29, 2026, where shareholders approved the company’s financial statements and independent auditor’s report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025. They also ratified an annual budget of US$29.9 million for aggregate compensation of directors and officers, underscoring continued investment in senior leadership.

At the same meeting, shareholders reappointed 11 directors, including Rubens Menin Teixeira de Souza and other existing board members, each to a new two-year term, ensuring continuity in corporate governance. The company also replaced its Third Amended and Restated Memorandum and Articles of Association with a Fourth Amended and Restated version, signaling an update to its governance framework that may refine shareholder rights and board powers going forward.

The most recent analyst rating on (INTR) stock is a Buy with a $11.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Inter & Company Incorporation Class A stock, see the INTR Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on INTR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, INTR is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by improving financial performance (turnaround to sustained profitability and solid cash generation) and a constructive earnings call outlook focused on ROE/efficiency gains. The main offsets are rising balance-sheet leverage and credit-risk/fee-pressure items flagged on the call, while technicals and valuation appear broadly neutral rather than strongly supportive.

To see Spark’s full report on INTR stock, click here.

More about Inter & Company Incorporation Class A

Inter & Co, Inc. is a Brazil-based financial services group listed on Nasdaq and B3 that operates primarily as a digital bank and financial platform. The company offers a range of banking, credit, investment and related services focused on retail and small-business clients, with a growing presence in both the Brazilian and international markets.

data.title: Centerra Gold’s Strong Q1 2026 Cash Generation Fuels Self-Funded Growth and Shareholder Returns
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Centerra Gold ( (TSE:CG) ) just unveiled an update.

Centerra Gold reported strong first-quarter 2026 results on April 29, 2026, with consolidated production of 68,001 ounces of gold and 14.2 million pounds of copper, and net earnings of $79.4 million, or $0.40 per share. The company’s cash balance rose to $543.5 million and total liquidity reached $943.5 million, even as it invested heavily in growth projects and returned $33 million to shareholders through buybacks and dividends.

Mount Milligan and Öksüt both performed in line with or ahead of plan, driving robust free cash flow that funded $69.4 million in capital spending, mainly on the Thompson Creek development and tailings work at Mount Milligan, while keeping all-in sustaining costs at $1,705 per ounce. Strategically, Centerra advanced its self-funded growth strategy with a positive Kemess economic study, a life-of-mine extension at Mount Milligan to 2045, continued build-out of the Goldfield project, and a provisional restart of the Langeloth facility in April 2026, underscoring its ambition to grow long-life, low-cost gold-copper production for shareholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:CG) stock is a Buy with a C$21.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Centerra Gold stock, see the TSE:CG Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CG Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CG is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by strong financial resilience (low leverage) and attractive valuation (low P/E). These positives are tempered by uneven free cash flow and mixed momentum signals, while the earnings call adds support from solid execution and liquidity but flags higher costs and increased capital requirements in 2026.

To see Spark’s full report on CG stock, click here.

More about Centerra Gold

Centerra Gold Inc. is a Canada-based gold and copper mining company listed on the TSX and NYSE, with core operations at the Mount Milligan copper-gold mine in British Columbia and the Öksüt gold mine in Türkiye. The company focuses on generating free cash flow from these cornerstone assets while advancing a pipeline of North American growth projects, including Kemess, Thompson Creek, Goldfield and the Langeloth metallurgical facility.

data.description:

The latest announcement is out from Kinross Gold ( (TSE:K) ).

Kinross Gold reported strong financial and operating results for the quarter ended March 31, 2026, highlighted by record free cash flow of about $837.5 million and record margins that rose 92% year-on-year and 22% sequentially, outpacing gains in the gold price. Production reached 492,563 gold-equivalent ounces, operating cash flow surpassed $1.1 billion, net earnings more than doubled versus Q1 2025, and cash on hand climbed to $2.2 billion, leaving the miner on track to meet its 2026 guidance targets.

The company intensified shareholder returns, distributing approximately $350 million so far in 2026 through dividends and share buybacks and topping $1 billion returned since April 2025, while its board declared another quarterly dividend of $0.04 per share. Operationally, flagship mines Paracatu and Tasiast delivered strong performance, and Kinross advanced key development assets including Great Bear, Lobo-Marte, and several U.S. projects, reinforcing its growth pipeline and cost position amid global uncertainty and elevated oil prices.

Kinross also emphasized disciplined cost management and hedging strategies that helped maintain an attractive relative cost base despite inflationary and fuel-price pressures. Management said sustained progress on environmental permitting, mine-life extension studies, and the forthcoming 2025 Sustainability Report underpins the company’s long-term production outlook and its commitment to responsible mining practices.

data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Delixy Holdings Limited ( (DLXY) ).

Delixy Holdings Limited, a Singapore-based oil trading company active across Southeast Asia, East Asia and the Middle East, concentrates on crude oil and a diversified range of oil-based products, supported by value-added logistics and trade finance services for its customers and suppliers. Its business model emphasizes strong regional relationships and tailored trading strategies in the oil markets.

On April 23, 2026, Delixy received a Nasdaq notice that its Class A ordinary shares had failed to meet the US$1.00 minimum bid price requirement for 30 consecutive business days, from March 11 to April 22, 2026. The shares remain listed on the Nasdaq Capital Market while the company has until October 20, 2026 to regain compliance, is monitoring its stock price, and may consider steps such as a reverse stock split, a development that could affect its capital structure and poses a potential delisting risk if unresolved.

The score is primarily constrained by financial performance: sharply higher leverage and a large drop in operating cash flow outweigh the modest 2024 revenue rebound and continued (but very thin) profitability. Technicals provide some near-term support with price above short-term averages and positive MACD, but the stock remains below the 100-day average. Valuation is neutral with a P/E around 20.8 and no dividend yield indicated.

To see Spark’s full report on DLXY stock, click here.

More about Delixy Holdings Limited

Delixy Holdings Limited is a Singapore-based company focused on trading oil-related products, including crude oil, fuel oil, motor gasoline, additives, gas oil, base oil, asphalt, naphtha and petrochemicals. Operating across Southeast Asia, East Asia and the Middle East, it leverages customer and supplier relationships, logistics support and financing capabilities to serve regional oil trading markets.

Anghami Inc. ( (ANGH) ) has provided an announcement.

On April 29, 2026, Anghami Inc. entered into a senior unsecured loan agreement with UBC Ventures W.L.L., an affiliate of OSN Streaming Limited, giving the company access to up to $20 million in advances that cannot be reborrowed once repaid. The facility, maturing on April 29, 2028, carries interest at six‑month Term SOFR plus 8%, is designated for general corporate and working capital purposes, and was approved by Anghami’s board following a special committee’s unanimous recommendation, signaling a move to bolster liquidity and operational flexibility without pledging collateral.

The loan’s unsecured nature and linkage to a strategic media affiliate may strengthen Anghami’s financial runway as it navigates a competitive streaming landscape in its core markets. By securing two-year funding at a relatively high, floating rate, the company trades higher financing costs for immediate balance sheet support, which could be important for sustaining platform investment and potential capital markets activity in a volatile funding environment.

data.title: Rezolve AI Presses Case for Commerce.com Tie-Up With Critical Flyer at Commerce Live
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Rezolve AI ( (RZLV) ) has shared an update.

Rezolve AI plc disclosed that on April 29, 2026 it distributed a flyer at the 2026 Commerce Live leadership conference in Chicago, where Commerce.com, Inc.’s leadership held an investor briefing. The flyer, furnished to investors via a Form 6-K, outlines Rezolve AI’s critique of Commerce.com’s performance and governance and promotes Rezolve’s proposed business combination with Commerce.com.

In the flyer, Rezolve AI highlights what it characterizes as a 96% decline in Commerce.com’s value from its post‑IPO peak, sluggish revenue growth, below-target net revenue retention and limited stock liquidity, and criticizes Commerce.com’s board for rejecting a proposal from a “fully funded acquirer” and adopting a poison pill. Rezolve argues that combining the two businesses could create a large-scale AI commerce platform serving about 60,000 storefronts with its Brain Suite, RezolvePay and brainpowa model, generating more than $700 million in combined revenue and high-margin software economics, positioning the merged entity to capture growth in AI-driven ecommerce.

Rezolve’s communication urges Commerce.com shareholders to question their board about its strategic decisions and the timeline to unlock value, casting Rezolve’s plan as a concrete route to participate in the forecast expansion of AI-driven ecommerce. The filing underscores that any transaction remains subject to negotiations, shareholder and regulatory approvals, and integration risks, while directing investors to future SEC filings for detailed information on any potential deal structure and terms.

The most recent analyst rating on (RZLV) stock is a Buy with a $15.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Rezolve AI stock, see the RZLV Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on RZLV Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, RZLV is a Neutral.

Overall score is held back primarily by weak financial performance—very large losses and sustained negative free cash flow—despite strong 2025 revenue growth and improved balance sheet footing. Technicals are moderately supportive with positive near-term momentum, while valuation is difficult to assess due to an uninformative P/E and no dividend yield data.

To see Spark’s full report on RZLV stock, click here.

More about Rezolve AI

Rezolve AI plc is a London-headquartered technology company focused on AI-driven advanced commerce solutions. Its offering includes the Brain Suite AI engine and RezolvePay payments platform, as well as a commerce-specific AI model, brainpowa, aimed at powering “agentic commerce” for retailers and ecommerce operators. The company positions itself to help merchants scale AI-enabled commerce as that segment of ecommerce grows.

Average Trading Volume: 17,806,789

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: $1.03B

data.title: Profusa Faces Nasdaq Listing Risk Amid Financing Amendments
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Profusa ( (PFSA) ) has provided an update.

On April 24, 2026, Profusa, Inc. amended a $2.5 million promissory note with NorthView Sponsor I LLC, confirming $1,869,796 in outstanding principal, eliminating interest, extending maturity to December 31, 2026, and granting the holder the right to convert the note into common stock at a price tied to market levels but not below $0.35. On April 29, 2026, the parties further amended the agreement to cap conversion-related share issuance at 19.99% of outstanding common stock absent shareholder approval, and to require Profusa to seek such approval within 90 days and at regular intervals thereafter if not obtained.

On April 28, 2026, Profusa disclosed it had failed to regain compliance with Nasdaq’s $15 million minimum market value of publicly held shares requirement by the April 27, 2026 deadline, adding to existing deficiencies on minimum bid price and total market value of listed securities. The Nasdaq Hearings Panel will review these issues together, and while Profusa plans a written response, there is no assurance its listing will be maintained, underscoring continued risk for shareholders.

Separately, on April 29, 2026, Profusa amended a warrant issued to Ascent Partners Fund LLC that allows the purchase of up to 3,333,333 common shares at $0.50 per share through 2031, deleting provisions that previously mandated automatic conversion or assumption of the warrant in certain fundamental transactions. The change removes built-in protections that would have guaranteed cash or marketable securities, or succession of the warrant, in major corporate events, potentially altering the warrant holder’s treatment in future deals while leaving other terms intact.

More about Profusa

Profusa, Inc. operates in the life sciences sector and is listed on the Nasdaq Global Market, where its capital structure includes common stock and various convertible and warrant instruments. The company’s financing arrangements involve promissory notes and equity-linked securities that are subject to Nasdaq listing rules and shareholder approval requirements.

Profusa’s capital markets strategy reflects an active use of convertible debt and warrants, highlighting its reliance on structured financing while it navigates multiple Nasdaq compliance challenges. Its shareholder base and prospective investors are directly affected by changes to conversion features, warrant terms, and ongoing risks to its exchange listing status.

Average Trading Volume: 5,148,228

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $2.26M

Telecom Argentina ( (TEO) ) has issued an update.

On April 29, 2026, Telecom Argentina held its ordinary, extraordinary and special shareholders’ meetings in Buenos Aires, approving the 2025 financial statements and other regulatory documentation despite reporting an inflation-adjusted retained loss. Shareholders endorsed absorbing AR$135.6 billion in negative retained earnings with existing voluntary reserves, reclassifying additional reserves to contributed surplus, and authorizing the board to distribute up to US$300 million in dividends before year-end 2026.

The meeting also ratified the performance and compensation of directors and supervisory committee members for fiscal 2025 and allowed fee advances for the 2026 period, signaling continuity in governance despite losses. Five independent regular and five alternate members were appointed to the Supervisory Committee for 2026, reinforcing regulatory compliance and oversight structures as the company manages its capital structure and potential shareholder returns.

data.title: Televisa Q1 2026: Profits Surge as Fiber and Enterprise Offset Satellite Decline
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Grupo Televisa, S.A.B. ( (TV) ).

On April 28, 2026, Televisa reported first-quarter 2026 results showing a 3.1% year-on-year revenue decline to Ps.14.5 billion, largely due to a steep drop in satellite services revenue tied to a 27.6% contraction in its revenue-generating units. Despite softer top-line performance, operating segment income rose 5.2% with margin expanding to 41.4%, while net income attributable to shareholders more than tripled to Ps.1.03 billion, helped by higher contributions from associates, lower other expenses and reduced taxes.

The company continued shifting its telecom mix toward higher-value connectivity, upgrading over 1.5 million homes to fiber-to-the-home, reaching more than 20 million homes passed, and posting gains in broadband and mobile subscribers that supported modest 0.9% residential revenue growth and a strong 30.0% jump in enterprise revenue. The contrasting trends underscore an ongoing strategic pivot away from declining satellite services toward fiber-based and enterprise solutions, a move that is helping sustain profitability and could reshape Televisa’s competitive position in Mexico’s telecom market despite rising finance costs.

The most recent analyst rating on (TV) stock is a Hold with a $3.70 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Grupo Televisa, S.A.B. stock, see the TV Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on TV Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, TV is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial performance (ongoing net losses and declining revenue) and bearish technical momentum (below key moving averages with weak RSI/MACD). Partially offsetting this are positive earnings-call signals around margin improvement, free cash flow and deleveraging, plus a moderate dividend yield, though guidance implies sustained elevated CapEx and the dividend suspension is a notable negative.

To see Spark’s full report on TV stock, click here.

More about Grupo Televisa, S.A.B.

Grupo Televisa, S.A.B. is a Mexico-based telecommunications and media company that now reports its Cable and Sky operations as a single Telecom segment, encompassing residential, satellite and enterprise services. Its core offerings include broadband, pay TV, mobile virtual network operations and enterprise connectivity, with a network that passes more than 20 million homes.

Average Trading Volume: 1,413,203

Current Market Cap: $1.41B

On April 29, 2026, Birkenstock Holding plc held its 2026 annual general meeting, where shareholders approved the company’s annual report and consolidated accounts for the fiscal year ended September 30, 2025, and confirmed EY GmbH & Co. data.title: Auddia Simplifies Capital Structure Through Preferred Stock Exchange
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Auddia ( (AUUD) ) is now available.

On September 30, 2024, Auddia Inc. entered into a financing transaction with accredited investors involving Series C convertible preferred stock and warrants, raising $1 million in gross proceeds by issuing 1,000 shares of Series C preferred stock at $1,000 per share. The securities in this private placement were offered and sold without registration under the Securities Act, relying on available exemptions from federal and state registration requirements.

On April 23, 2026, the company further restructured its capital by entering into an exchange agreement with those investors to swap 750 outstanding Series C preferred shares, including accrued dividends, for 216,525 shares of common stock at an exchange price of $3.91 per share. Following this exchange, no Series C preferred stock remained outstanding, simplifying Auddia’s capital structure and potentially improving common equity liquidity for existing and new shareholders.

More about Auddia

Auddia Inc. is a publicly traded company that raises capital through securities offerings, including preferred stock and warrants, to support its operations and growth. The company engages accredited investors in private financing transactions under exemptions from U.S. securities registration requirements, reflecting a reliance on capital markets outside of public offerings.

Average Trading Volume: 1,839,747

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $751.3K

data.title: Tronox Shareholders Back Board, Pay and Capital Flexibility
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

TRONOX ( (TROX) ) has issued an announcement.

At its Annual General Meeting of Shareholders held on April 28, 2026, Tronox shareholders elected all nominated directors to terms expiring in 2027 and gave strong majority support to executive compensation in both U.S. and U.K. advisory votes. Investors also ratified the appointment and re-appointment of PricewaterhouseCoopers as auditor for U.S. and U.K. purposes and approved receipt of the company’s U.K. audited accounts and related reports for the year ended December 31, 2025.

Shareholders authorized the board to allot shares, including without preemptive rights, endorsed forms of share repurchase contracts and counterparties, and backed an amendment to increase authorized shares under the Management Equity Incentive Plan. The broad approval across governance, pay, capital authorities, and buyback mechanisms reinforces the board’s flexibility in capital management and signals continued shareholder support for Tronox’s current leadership and financial policies.

The most recent analyst rating on (TROX) stock is a Sell with a $8.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on TRONOX stock, see the TROX Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on TROX Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, TROX is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial performance (losses, compressed margins, and negative free cash flow alongside elevated leverage). Technical momentum is a meaningful offset with the stock trading above key moving averages and a positive MACD, while valuation is mixed due to a loss-driven negative P/E despite a modest dividend. Earnings-call guidance improves the outlook but remains execution-dependent given recent large losses and debt constraints.

To see Spark’s full report on TROX stock, click here.

More about TRONOX

Tronox Holdings plc is a global chemicals company focused on the production and marketing of titanium dioxide pigment and related mineral sands, which are used in products such as paints, plastics, and paper. The company operates in a highly competitive industrial and specialty chemicals market, serving manufacturers worldwide that rely on consistent pigment quality and supply for their end-use applications.

data.title: Assertio Extends Garda Tender Offer Commencement Deadline
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Assertio Therapeutics ( (ASRT) ) has provided an update.

On April 29, 2026, Assertio announced that it had mutually agreed with Garda Therapeutics to extend to May 4, 2026 the deadline to commence Garda’s previously announced tender offer to acquire all outstanding Assertio shares. The extension follows the April 8, 2026 definitive merger agreement under which Garda plans to acquire Assertio for $18.00 per share in cash, valuing the deal at about $125.1 million plus a contingent value right.

The merger agreement includes a 20-day window-shop period that allows Assertio to engage with other potential bidders that might deliver superior value to shareholders. If the board terminates the Garda deal in favor of a higher offer during this period, a reduced breakup fee would apply, preserving flexibility for investors while maintaining a structured path toward a potential sale of the company.

The most recent analyst rating on (ASRT) stock is a Hold with a $18.05 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Assertio Therapeutics stock, see the ASRT Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on ASRT Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, ASRT is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak financial performance—declining revenue, ongoing losses, and a 2025 shift to negative operating/free cash flow. Technicals are moderately supportive with the stock above key moving averages and positive MACD. The earnings outlook is constructive on FY2026 guidance and Rolvedon-driven profitability expansion, while the acquisition event adds meaningful deal-value support that partially offsets the fundamental and cash-flow risk.

To see Spark’s full report on ASRT stock, click here.

More about Assertio Therapeutics

Assertio Holdings, Inc. is a pharmaceutical company with commercial capabilities focused on marketing differentiated therapies, primarily in the oncology market. The company’s portfolio is positioned to address patients’ needs in cancer care, where it competes in a specialized segment of the broader biopharmaceutical industry.

data.title: Anteris Ends v2vmedtech Development Agreement, Limits Exposure
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Anteris Technologies Global Corp. ( (AVR) ) is now available.

On April 28, 2026, Anteris Technologies Global Corp., via its subsidiary, elected to discontinue further development contributions under its April 18, 2023 Contribution and Stock Purchase Agreement with v2vmedtech, inc., after completing Stage 1 and during Stage 2 of the program. As part of this decision, Anteris must pay v2v a $400,000 break fee to support continued technology development, and the related Development Agreement will terminate upon payment.

Following the notice, Anteris has no further funding obligations to v2v, while v2v’s initial shareholders may either buy out Anteris’ entire equity stake for the amount of its aggregate contributions to date or dilute it to a capped minority stake as set out in the agreement. The company has indicated it does not expect this withdrawal and the termination of the development arrangements to have a material adverse effect on its consolidated financial position or liquidity, suggesting limited balance-sheet impact from the strategic pullback.

The most recent analyst rating on (AVR) stock is a Buy with a $17.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Anteris Technologies Global Corp. stock, see the AVR Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on AVR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, AVR is a Neutral.

The score is primarily held down by very weak financial performance (declining revenue, large recurring losses, negative equity, and heavy cash burn). Technicals are neutral and provide limited support, while valuation is constrained by losses and lack of dividend. A positive operational event (new facility lease with abatements) helps modestly but does not offset the elevated financing risk.

To see Spark’s full report on AVR stock, click here.

More about Anteris Technologies Global Corp.

Anteris Technologies Global Corp., through its wholly owned subsidiary Anteris Technologies Corporation, operates in the medical technology sector, pursuing development-stage collaborations to advance specialized healthcare technologies. The company structures its investments via staged contribution agreements that can be scaled back or exited based on predefined milestones and strategic considerations.

data.title: Fat Brands Appoints Restructuring Expert as Interim CEO
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Fat Brands ( (FATAQ) ) has provided an update.

Effective April 29, 2026, Fat Brands and affiliate TWNP appointed restructuring specialist Keshav Lall as interim chief executive officer, replacing the former CEO who remains on leave under a mediated agreement. On the same date, Lall was also named interim CEO of certain direct and indirect subsidiaries, consolidating executive oversight across parts of the corporate structure.

Lall, a founding partner of financial advisory firm Uzzi & Lall and former Essar Capital Americas chairman and CEO, brings extensive experience in managing financial stress, restructurings, and creditor negotiations to the role. Under an engagement letter dated April 26, 2026, Uzzi & Lall will be paid $100,000 per month for providing his services, signaling a board focus on stabilizing operations and navigating ongoing financial or operational challenges without related-party conflicts.

The most recent analyst rating on (FATAQ) stock is a Buy with a $10.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Fat Brands stock, see the FATAQ Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on FATAQ Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, FATAQ is a Neutral.

The score is primarily weighed down by weak financial performance (losses, negative equity, and negative cash flow) and bankruptcy-related corporate events. Technicals also remain bearish. Earnings call initiatives and cost actions provide only limited offset given ongoing debt and profitability challenges.

To see Spark’s full report on FATAQ stock, click here.

More about Fat Brands

Fat Brands operates in the restaurant and franchising industry, focused on acquiring, developing, and managing a portfolio of quick-service, fast-casual, and casual dining brands. The company generates revenue primarily through franchising fees and royalties from its branded restaurant concepts, targeting both domestic and international markets through an asset-light growth model.

data.title: FMC Shareholders Weigh Governance Changes at 2026 Annual Meeting
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from FMC ( (FMC) ).

FMC held its 2026 annual shareholders’ meeting on April 28, 2026, with approximately 85% of outstanding shares represented in person or by proxy. Shareholders elected nine directors, each to serve a one-year term expiring in 2027, underscoring continuity in the company’s board leadership.

Investors ratified KPMG LLP as FMC’s independent auditor for fiscal 2026 and gave non-binding approval to the company’s executive compensation program. However, several governance-driven charter amendments—eliminating supermajority voting provisions and granting shareholders a 25% special-meeting right—failed to obtain the required 80% supermajority, while miscellaneous charter changes and a new 2026 incentive stock plan were approved, signaling selective support for governance and compensation changes.

The most recent analyst rating on (FMC) stock is a Hold with a $16.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on FMC stock, see the FMC Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on FMC Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, FMC is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak financial performance (losses, higher leverage, and negative operating/free cash flow). Offsetting factors include a neutral-to-slightly improving technical picture, a high dividend yield (tempered by loss-making earnings), and an earnings call that pairs near-term headwinds with a clear deleveraging and cost-reduction plan.

To see Spark’s full report on FMC stock, click here.

The latest update is out from Myseum.AI ( (MYSE) ).

On April 28, 2026, Myseum.AI, Inc. appointed Ian Goldberg as Head of Marketing, Partnerships & Monetization, a new role intended to support the company’s AI expansion and drive user adoption of its Picture Party social media platform. Goldberg, former founder and CEO of youth sports technology firm iSport360 and a recognized digital marketing innovator, is expected to leverage his experience in scaling content platforms and user engagement to accelerate Myseum.AI’s growth and strengthen its position in next-generation, privacy-first social networking.

More about Myseum.AI

Myseum.AI, Inc. is a privacy-focused AI and social media technology company that develops platforms for secure digital sharing and storage. Its flagship product, the Picture Party platform, offers a patented instant social networking experience that enables users to create curated albums, build encrypted galleries with controlled access, personalize content feeds, and organize collections, and is available on iOS and Android app stores.
